samples.xml_description:
<module id="traffic_light">
    <description>Implement a traffic light with red, yellow, green indicators and a pedestrian button. In normal traffic light operation, the east-west (EW) light should have a 60-clock cycle period, with 5 clock cycles of yellow and 10 clock cycles of red. The north-south (NS) light should have a 60-clock cycle period, with 5 clock cycles of yellow and 10 clock cycles of red. When the pedestrian button is pressed, if the remaining EW green time is greater than 10 clocks, force it to be 10 clocks. If the remaining EW green time is less than 10 clocks, do not change the remaining time. The EW and NS lights are paired. When the EW light is green, the NS light is red. When the EW light is red, the NS light is green.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="rst_n">Reset signal (active low).</input>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal.</input>
        <input id="pass_request">Pedestrian button input.</input>
        <output id="red">Red output signal.</output>
        <output id="yellow">Yellow output signal.</output>
        <output id="green">Green output signal.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <enum id="idle">Idle state.</enum>
        <enum id="s1_red">State 1 red.</enum>
        <enum id="s2_yellow">State 2 yellow.</enum>
        <enum id="s3_green">State 3 green.</enum>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description>
        <sequential_logic id="state">
            <assign>
                <switch>
                    <case condition="idle">Set state to s1_red.</case>
                    <case condition="s1_red">Set state to s3_green after 5 clock cycles.</case>
                    <case condition="s2_yellow">Set state to s1_red after 5 clock cycles.</case>
                    <case condition="s3_green">Set state to s2_yellow after 5 clock cycles.</case>
                </switch>
            </assign>
        </sequential_logic>
        <sequential_logic id="cnt">
            <assign>
                <switch>
                    <case condition="!rst_n">Set cnt to 10.</case>
                    <case condition="pass_request && green">Set cnt to 10.</case>
                    <case condition="!green && p_green">Set cnt to 60.</case>
                    <case condition="!yellow && p_yellow">Set cnt to 5.</case>
                    <case condition="!red && p_red">Set cnt to 10.</case>
                    <case condition="default">Set cnt to cnt - 1.</case>
                </switch>
            </assign>
        </sequential_logic>
        <sequential_logic id="red">
            <assign>
                <switch>
                    <case condition="!rst_n">Set red to 0.</case>
                    <case condition="state==s1_red">Set red to 1.</case>
                    <case condition="state==s3_green">Set red to 0.</case>
                    <case condition="default">Set red to red.</case>
                </switch>
            </assign>
        </sequential_logic>
        <sequential_logic id="yellow">
            <assign>
                <switch>
                    <case condition="!rst_n">Set yellow to 0.</case>
                    <case condition="state==s2_yellow">Set yellow to 1.</case>
                    <case condition="state==s1_red">Set yellow to 0.</case>
                    <case condition="default">Set yellow to yellow.</case>
                </switch>
            </assign>
        </sequential_logic>
        <sequential_logic id="green">
            <assign>
                <switch>
                    <case condition="!rst_n">Set green to 0.</case>
                    <case condition="state==s3_green">Set green to 1.</case>
                    <case condition="state==s2_yellow">Set green to 0.</case>
                    <case condition="default">Set green to green.</case>
                </switch>
            </assign>
        </sequential_logic>
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>Implement a traffic light controller. The first always block is the state transition logic. Use a case statement to handle the state transitions. The second always block is the counting logic. The counter is a 8-bit register. The counter is decremented by 1 on every positive edge of the clock or negative edge of the reset signal. If the reset signal is not active, the counter is decremented. If the counter reaches 0, the state transitions to the next state. The counter is set based on the state. For example, in the s1_red state, the counter is set to 3. In the s2_yellow state, the counter is set to 3. In the s3_green state, the counter is set to 3. The third always block is the output logic. Assign the output based on the state.</implementation>
</module>
